title: Athyrium multidentatum
taxonomy: Athyrium multidentatum is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is classified as a type of lady fern. The classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. Additionally, it falls under the order Polypodiales and the family Athyriaceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.
uses: Athyrium multidentatum is a species of fern known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant contains various bioactive compounds that may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the fronds of Athyrium multidentatum have been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make teas, infusions, and poultices for their perceived therapeutic benefits. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal potential of Athyrium multidentatum and to ensure its safe use as a medicinal agent.

constituents: Athyrium multidentatum is known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its potential health benefits. intro: Athyrium multidentatum, also known as the many-toothed lady fern, is a species of flowering plant in the fern family, native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium multidentatum include various flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and cardiovascular disorders.
